verify-when-job-scheduling.script:
assert_exists() {
  local file="$1"
  test -f "$file" || {
    echo "[ERROR] missing expected marker: $file"
    exit 1
  }
}

assert_absent() {
  local file="$1"
  if [ -e "$file" ]; then
    echo "[ERROR] unexpected marker present: $file"
    exit 1
  fi
}

assert_contains() {
  local file="$1"
  local expected="$2"
  grep -F "$expected" "$file" >/dev/null || {
    echo "[ERROR] expected '$expected' in $file"
    exit 1
  }
}

assert_exists reports/success-anchor.log
assert_exists reports/run-on-success-before-failure.log
assert_exists reports/run-always-before-failure.log
assert_exists reports/trigger-pipeline-failure.log
assert_exists reports/run-on-failure-after-failure.log
assert_exists reports/run-always-after-failure.log

assert_absent reports/skip-on-failure-before-failure.log
assert_absent reports/skip-on-success-after-failure.log

assert_contains reports/success-anchor.log 'success-anchor:ran'
assert_contains reports/run-on-success-before-failure.log 'run-on-success-before-failure:ran'
assert_contains reports/run-always-before-failure.log 'run-always-before-failure:ran'
assert_contains reports/trigger-pipeline-failure.log 'trigger-pipeline-failure:about-to-fail'
assert_contains reports/run-on-failure-after-failure.log 'run-on-failure-after-failure:ran'
assert_contains reports/run-always-after-failure.log 'run-always-after-failure:ran'
